banner.insecure_body: this dashboard is not using TLS. Traffic can be read or modified on the network path. Use --https-cert-file / --https-key-file or terminate TLS on a reverse proxy. See GET /status (dashboard_transport).
banner.gov_body: severity {severity} (license {license_state}). Global API key surface: {mode}. Per-route RBAC is not implemented yet — see GET /status (enterprise_surface).

assessment.pack_intro: Below is a YAML-loaded placeholder questionnaire (generic copy in the public repo). Replace with a private or licensed pack via config.
assessment.pack_note: Submitted answers are stored in the local SQLite database (same file as scan results) for this POC.
assessment.summary_rows_stored: Stored {count} answer row(s) for this submission.
assessment.summary_score_none: No rubric weights are defined in the YAML pack (all scores zero); add optional per-question scores to enable a percentage.
assessment.summary_disclaimer: Outputs are self-reported signals only — not legal advice, audit, or certification. Keep proprietary questionnaire text in a private pack.
assessment.export_contract: Exports use this authenticated download URL (attachment). There is no separate on-disk export path in the product; redirect curl or your browser to save a file. When api.require_api_key is enabled, send X-API-Key or Authorization: Bearer like other dashboard requests.
assessment.history_intro: Stored batches in this SQLite database (newest first). Open a summary or export; highlighted row matches the batch in the URL.
assessment.summary_integrity_detail: {ok} verified · {mismatch} failed verification · {unsealed} without seal · {unknown} sealed but key unavailable
assessment.summary_integrity_none: No HMAC secret was configured when saving; rows are stored without seals.
assessment.lead: This page is an optional product placeholder. The open repository does not ship proprietary questionnaire items.
assessment.body_p1: Scope and architecture options are described in docs/plans/PLAN_MATURITY_SELF_ASSESSMENT_GRC_QUESTIONNAIRE.md.
assessment.body_p2: Any future output is a self-reported maturity signal only—not legal advice, audit, or certification.

dashboard.scan_compressed: Also scan inside compressed files (zip, tar, 7z, …)
dashboard.scan_compressed_hint: May significantly increase run time and I/O. Use only when you need to inspect contents of archives.
dashboard.content_type: Use content-type detection (magic bytes) for file format
dashboard.content_type_hint: Helps find renamed or cloaked files (e.g. PDF saved as .txt). Reads the start of each file—may increase I/O and run time.
dashboard.jurisdiction: Heuristic jurisdiction hints (Report info — DPO / counsel)
dashboard.jurisdiction_hint: Optional notes when metadata may suggest CCPA/CPRA, Colorado, or Japan APPI scope. Not a legal conclusion; configure report.jurisdiction_hints for defaults.
dashboard.chart_intro: Total findings and risk score (0–100) per scan, in chronological order.
dashboard.chart_empty: Run at least one scan to see the progress graph.
dashboard.chart_aria: Findings and score over sequential scans
dashboard.no_sessions_row: No sessions yet. Start a scan.
dashboard.about_app_p: Reports (Excel and heatmap) and this dashboard are generated by this application; see About for full details.

config.intro: Edit the audit configuration (YAML). Saved file:
config.intro_tail: Changes apply to the next scan.
config.readme_blurb: See README and docs/USAGE.md for target types (database, filesystem, api, powerbi, dataverse, shares) and options.

reports.intro: Download Excel reports (and heatmap) for each scan session. “Download” regenerates the report for that session.
reports.no_sessions: No sessions yet. Run a scan from the

about.license_intro: Operational status of optional commercial enforcement (default: open distribution). See docs/LICENSING_SPEC.md.

help.what_p1: This application scans configured data sources (databases, filesystems, APIs and shares) for personal and sensitive data, stores only metadata in a local SQLite database, and generates Excel reports plus a sensitivity/risk heatmap PNG.
help.what_p2: Each run is a scan session with optional tenant / customer and technician / operator tags, so you can attribute results.
help.run_web_li2: Ensure your Configuration (see Config page) has the desired targets (databases, filesystems, APIs, etc.). The scan will run exactly those targets.
help.run_web_li3: (Optional) Fill in Customer / tenant and Technician / operator.
help.run_web_li4: Click Start scan. This triggers a full audit of all targets in the current config. The status panel will update and the page will auto-refresh when the run finishes.
help.run_web_tail: Recent sessions are listed at the bottom of the dashboard and on the Reports page, with download links.
help.run_cli_p1: From the repo root, prefer uv run python main.py (or activate your venv and run python main.py). Full flag list: python main.py --help. Packaged installs: man 1 data_boar (command) and man 5 data_boar (config file format).
help.run_cli_oneshot: One-shot audit (same targets as a dashboard scan):
help.run_cli_api: Start the API / dashBOARd (no scan until you use the browser or HTTP):
help.run_cli_tls: You must either terminate TLS at the process (--https-cert-file + --https-key-file, or the same under api: in config) or explicitly accept plaintext with --allow-insecure-http (or api.allow_insecure_http: true). The official Docker image passes --allow-insecure-http by default; mount certs and remove it for HTTPS.
help.run_cli_bind: Bind order when using --web: --host overrides api.host in config and API_HOST; if none are set, the default is 127.0.0.1 (Docker image often sets API_HOST=0.0.0.0). Port: api.port in config vs --port. Transport: GET /status and GET /health include dashboard_transport — see docs/USAGE.md.
help.run_cli_danger: Dangerous maintenance (lab/demo only): --reset-data wipes SQLite sessions/findings and deletes generated reports under report.output_dir. See SECURITY.md before using in production.
help.run_cli_audit: Audit trail export (CLI only): --export-audit-trail or --export-audit-trail path.json writes a JSON snapshot (wipe log, session summary; future: integrity rows). Does not modify the database. Omit the path or use - for stdout.
help.dl_li1: Use the Reports page to see all sessions and click Download to get the Excel report for a specific run.
help.dl_li2_intro: Use the API to script downloads:
help.cfg_p1: The configuration file (YAML or JSON) defines which targets are scanned. Minimal YAML example:
help.cfg_tail: Place this file alongside the application (default name config.yaml) or set the CONFIG_PATH environment variable to point to a different location.
help.rate_p1: To avoid accidental overload (for example, many scans triggered in a row or too many sessions in parallel via the API/dashboard), you can enable a rate_limit block in the main config:
help.rate_tail: When enabled, API endpoints that start scans may return HTTP 429 with a JSON body indicating why the request was rate-limited (too many running scans, or minimum interval not elapsed). The CLI only prints warnings using the same rules. See docs/USAGE.md (EN), docs/USAGE.pt_BR.md (pt-BR) and docs/data_boar.5 for details.
help.sense_p1: The app detects personal and sensitive data using regex, ML (TF-IDF + classifier), and optional DL (sentence embeddings). Out-of-the-box it recognises PII (CPF, email, phone, etc.) and sensitive categories (health, religion, political affiliation, gender, biometric, genetic, race, union, PEP, sex life). You can:
help.sense_li1: Add your own terms via ml_patterns_file or sensitivity_detection.ml_terms in config.
help.sense_li2: Use the example file docs/sensitivity_terms_sensitive_categories.example.yaml and set ml_patterns_file (or dl_patterns_file) to it.
help.sense_li3: Customise the Recommendations sheet with report.recommendation_overrides: map norm_tag patterns (e.g. health, religious, political) to Base legal, Risk, Priority, and “Relevante para”.
help.sense_tail: See docs/SENSITIVITY_DETECTION.md (EN) and docs/SENSITIVITY_DETECTION.pt_BR.md (pt-BR); docs/plans/completed/PLAN_SENSITIVE_CATEGORIES_ML_DL.md for design; docs/USAGE.md (EN) and docs/USAGE.pt_BR.md (pt-BR) for full recommendation_overrides examples.
help.sec_p1: The API and dashboard send security headers on every response (Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, HSTS when HTTPS). For deployment hardening (Docker, Kubernetes, non-root, NetworkPolicy, reverse proxy), see:
help.sec_li1: SECURITY.md – HTTP security headers and optional API key
help.sec_h3: What to keep an eye out for (technicians)
help.sec_t1: Passwords with special characters: Database and MongoDB passwords containing @, :, / or # are supported; the app encodes them in connection URLs automatically.
help.sec_t2: Protecting the Configuration page: The config file may contain credentials. If the API is exposed to untrusted users or networks, set api.require_api_key: true and use a strong API key so only authenticated requests can access /config.
help.sec_t3: Deployment: Run behind a reverse proxy with TLS when the dashboard or API is exposed to the internet; use the optional API key and rate limiting as an extra layer.
help.sec_tail: For a full list of security fixes, regression tests, and recommendations, see docs/SECURITY.md (EN) and docs/SECURITY.pt_BR.md (pt-BR).
help.more_p1: For full details, including optional connectors (Power BI, Dataverse, REST APIs, shares) and advanced tuning, see:
help.more_li2: docs/SENSITIVITY_DETECTION.md (EN) · docs/SENSITIVITY_DETECTION.pt_BR.md (pt-BR) – ML/DL terms and config
help.more_tail: These files live in the project repository and should be kept in sync with the behaviour you see here.

login.disabled: WebAuthn is not enabled in this deployment. Set api.webauthn.enabled and the token secret environment variable before starting the process.
login.intro: Use a passkey (WebAuthn) to access the dashboard after the first passkey is registered. If none exist yet, open this page and choose Register passkey.
login.hint_https: WebAuthn requires HTTPS or localhost. Match api.webauthn.origin and rp_id to the browser URL.

js.err_scan_progress_guide: Wait for the current scan to finish, or restart the API if it is stuck.
js.err_rate_guide: Wait and try again, or adjust rate_limit.max_concurrent_scans and min_interval_seconds in config.
js.err_auth_guide: Check API key or auth configuration if the API is protected.
js.err_network_guide: Request did not reach the server. Check network, CORS, or ad-blockers; ensure the API is running.
js.err_server_guide: Server error. Check API logs and try again.
js.retry_after: Retry after {n} seconds, or adjust rate_limit in config.
